content.problemStatement:
Tanny and Purgi are two very good friends. They went to a nice restaurant for some good dinner after a long taxing week. Tanny has n banknotes of value a1,a2,a3,a4,......,ana_1,a_2,a_3,a_4,......,a_na1​,a2​,a3​,a4​,......,an​ dollars. Similarly Purgi has m banknotes of values b1,b2,b3,b4,........,bmb_1,b_2,b_3,b_4,........,b_mb1​,b2​,b3​,b4​,........,bm​ dollars.

Their bill came out to be CCC dollars. Is it possible for them to pay the bill if both of them can just pay with exactly 1 banknote?


Input Format

The first line will have 3 integers n,m,cn,m,cn,m,c separated by a space.
The second and third line will contain the values of the banknotes Tanny and Purgi (separated by a space) respectively.


Output Format
Output YESYESYES if it is possible to pay the bill using just 2 banknotes ( one from Purgi and one from Tanny).
Else output NONONO.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains three integers n, m and c (1 ≤ n, m ≤ 10^5, 1 ≤ c ≤ 2·10^9).
- The second line contains n integers a1 … an (0 ≤ ai ≤ 10^9).
- The third line contains m integers b1 … bm (0 ≤ bi ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if there exists a banknote from Tanny and a banknote from Purgi whose values sum exactly to c, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Tanny has a 1‑dollar note and Purgi has a 33‑dollar note; 1 + 33 = 34, so the bill can be paid.
All possible sums are 5,6,7,8,9; none equals 10.
Both friends can use a 0‑dollar note, 0 + 0 = 0.

solution.approach:
For each test case we need to know whether there exist i and j such that ai + bj = c.
1. Insert all values of Tanny's banknotes (array a) into an unordered hash set.
2. For each value bj of Purgi, check whether (c - bj) exists in the set.
3. If any check succeeds, answer is YES; otherwise NO.
The hash set gives O(1) average lookup, so the whole test case runs in O(n + m) time.
Edge cases such as zero‑valued notes are naturally handled because 0 is a valid key in the set.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n + m) per test case, where n and m are the numbers of banknotes of Tanny and Purgi respectively.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the hash set storing Tanny's banknotes.
